steps.title: Identify the structures on each side of the punctuation
steps.content: On the left of the punctuation choice: “significance” serves as the object of the verb “held” in a relative clause. On the right: “depictions of birds, horses, and other animals” is a noun phrase that is coordinate with the preceding content in a larger series.

steps.title: Apply the rule for series punctuation
steps.content:
A semicolon separates complex items in a series, and here it correctly marks the boundary between the first item (“patterns which held symbolic significance”) and the second item (“depictions of birds, horses, and other animals”).

The comma blurs this boundary, the period inappropriately fragments the list, and the colon suggests elaboration rather than separation.

That is the correct answer.
